college.institution_type: Indian Institute of Technology; autonomous statutory institute and deemed university
college.address: IIT Bombay, Powai, Mumbai 400076, Maharashtra, India

accreditation.notes: No current institute-specific approval record was verified; statutory IIT status should not be replaced by an inference.

admission_routes.qualifying_exam: JEE Main qualification followed by JEE Advanced under the current official rules
admission_routes.admission_process: Qualify JEE Advanced, register and fill choices in JoSAA, receive seat allocation, complete online reporting and IIT Bombay admission formalities.
admission_routes.board_eligibility: As prescribed in the official JEE Advanced 2026 information brochure; final verification required.
admission_routes.category_reservation: OPEN, GEN-EWS, OBC-NCL, SC, ST and PwD pools as published in the JoSAA matrix.
admission_routes.foreign_national_route: Separate JEE Advanced/foreign-national rules may apply; verify the current brochure.
admission_routes.special_round: CSAB Special Rounds do not allocate IIT seats.
admission_routes.branch_change_rule: No provision for branch change for JoSAA-admitted programmes with effect from academic year 2023-24; other conversions remain governed by prevailing rules.

senior_junior_culture.summary: The official Student Mentor Programme and hostel councils provide structured peer interaction. Independent recent evidence is too limited to generalise about hierarchy, mentorship quality or fresher inclusion across the institute.
positive_themes: Hostel councils led by senior students
Large student-run technical and cultural ecosystem
negative_themes: No sufficiently broad recent independent evidence on unhealthy hierarchy or day-to-day senior accessibility
senior_junior_culture.evidence_strength: Limited anecdotal signal plus institution-associated structure

academic_culture.summary: Official material describes a rigorous credit-based semester system with minors, honours, projects and strong faculty interaction. Student discussions indicate high workload and competition, but individual experience varies by programme and course load.
positive_themes: Minors and honours provide curricular breadth
CSE focus areas and technical clubs support specialised learning
negative_themes: High workload and performance pressure are recurrent anecdotal themes
No branch change for JoSAA programmes from 2023-24

hostel_reality.summary: IIT Bombay is residential and official sources document hostel internet, messes, laundry and recreation. Room allocation, occupancy and condition vary by hostel and batch; videos and forum posts cannot support a single institute-wide quality rating.
negative_themes: Facilities differ across old and newer hostels
Student reports on conditions are not sufficiently consistent for a quality score

club_and_campus_life.summary: Current official pages show a broad and active technical, cultural, sports, media and entrepreneurship ecosystem. Recruitment processes often involve orientations, assignments and interviews; fairness and accessibility were not independently evaluated.
positive_themes: Large technical and cultural umbrella councils
Broad first-year project exposure through some clubs
negative_themes: Competition for leadership roles and selection experience are not independently documented
Latest activity dates are unavailable for some organisations

coding_and_placement_culture.summary: Official placement reports and WnCC activity indicate a strong coding, analytics, finance, consulting and core-engineering ecosystem. Company-wise roles and branch eligibility are not publicly complete, and forum concerns should not replace official statistics.
positive_themes: High number of accepted placement offers
negative_themes: Current company-wise roles, packages and branch eligibility are incomplete
Non-CSE access to individual software roles cannot be assumed
Public placement sentiment is mixed during weaker markets
coding_and_placement_culture.evidence_strength: Strong official outcome signal; limited independent process evidence

locality_and_student_life.summary: The Powai campus combines a residential environment with access to Mumbai’s corporate, research and startup ecosystem. Travel is supported by suburban rail, buses, taxis and autorickshaws, but Mumbai traffic and off-campus living costs are meaningful constraints.
positive_themes: Nearest suburban station at Kanjur Marg
locality_and_student_life.evidence_strength: Moderate official/map-derived signal with limited anecdotes

overall_satisfaction.summary: Available evidence suggests IIT Bombay is best suited to self-directed students seeking rigorous academics, strong peers, wide extracurricular choice and exposure to Mumbai. Students expecting low pressure, guaranteed branch mobility, uniform hostel quality or effortless placement may find the experience less suitable.
negative_themes: Admission does not guarantee a placement, scholarship, preferred role or branch change

parent_mode_summary.label: Perspective summary based on collected evidence
parent_mode_summary.summary: IIT Bombay has authoritative 2026 admissions, seat and fee data, strong historical placement outcomes and extensive on-campus facilities. The current calculated four-year institutional/hostel/mess cost for a full-tuition-paying Indian student is about INR 11.96 lakh before personal expenses and future revisions. Scholarships and remissions can materially reduce tuition but are not guaranteed. The main unresolved parent-facing issues are exact hostel room allocation, final 2025-26 placement outcomes, company-wise employment details and the campus-area source conflict.
priority_points: Verify income/category fee remission before payment
Do not interpret CTC as guaranteed take-home salary
Use official medical and wellness contacts
Budget for Mumbai travel and personal costs beyond institute charges

student_mode_summary.label: Perspective summary based on collected evidence
student_mode_summary.summary: IIT Bombay offers 16 JoSAA programmes, no branch change from 2023-24, a strong technical/cultural ecosystem, a residential campus and broad placement sectors. Students should choose the allotted branch on its own merits, use minors/honours and clubs for exploration, and build role-specific skills rather than assuming every recruiter is open to every branch. Hostel experience varies across buildings and workload can be demanding.
priority_points: Choose branch without relying on later branch change
Use first two years to explore minors, honours, clubs and research
Verify each employer’s branch and CPI rules during placement season
Treat student reviews as anecdotes, not guarantees
Plan for high academic workload and competitive extracurricular selections

decision_risks.risk: Selecting a branch while expecting a later branch change
decision_risks.analysis: Official policy states there is no branch change for JoSAA-admitted programmes from 2023-24.

decision_risks.risk: Treating placement CTC or institute averages as a guaranteed personal outcome
decision_risks.analysis: Reports cover accepted offers across many programmes and sectors; company/role/branch-level outcomes are not publicly complete.

decision_risks.risk: Assuming a uniform hostel room or quality standard
decision_risks.analysis: Hostels vary by age, room allocation and batch; official common facilities do not establish uniform quality.

decision_risks.risk: Underbudgeting because future semester, mess and personal costs can change
decision_risks.analysis: Four/five-year totals are calculated from current 2026-27 charges and are not official fixed programme totals.

decision_risks.risk: Assuming scholarship or fee remission is automatic
decision_risks.analysis: Benefits require income/category/merit verification and may have award limits and renewal conditions.

decision_risks.risk: Using isolated online opinions as campus-wide truth
decision_risks.analysis: Public student evidence is limited, self-selected and sometimes conflicting.

data_gaps: Official 2026 JoSAA cutoffs are not yet available; historical 2023-2025 cutoffs are provided without prediction labels.
Final 2025-26 placement report is not publicly available in the collected evidence.
Company-wise current recruiters, roles, branch eligibility, selection counts and packages are not publicly complete.
Current AICTE, NAAC and programme-level NBA applicability/records were not verified.
Uniform hostel room occupancy, allotment guarantee, curfew and current building-wise charges/rules are not fully documented.
Current activity dates and recruitment processes for some clubs require manual recheck.
Independent student evidence is insufficient for numeric campus-life, mess, faculty-accessibility or senior-junior scores.
Live map travel times and Mumbai public-transport routes can change.
